trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'With their guild shunned for opening the doors to Phyrexia, some among the Golgari embraced their status as pariahs and took out their bitterness on all who crossed their paths.' [Scryfall] This references the Golgari Swarm's deep compleation during the March of the Machine Phyrexian invasion — Vraska, the Golgari guildmaster, had been Phyrexian-influenced and led many Golgari into compleation. The guild suffered severe casualties and social ostracism on Ravnica in the aftermath. [WebSearch snippet: Murders at Karlov Manor lore summary]

Undercity Eliminator is typed Creature — Gorgon Assassin, placing this unnamed figure in the broader Golgari gorgon tradition. Gorgons are recurring Golgari creatures in Ravnica lore — Jarad's advisor Varolz and the gorgon community of the undercity are canonical presences. The 'eliminator' role in the flavor text is explicit: a guild pariah repurposing bitterness as violence against anyone in their path. [Scryfall]

The enter trigger allows sacrificing an artifact or creature to exile a target creature an opponent controls — a flexible removal effect that works as both a sacrifice outlet and an exile answer. Legal in Standard through Vintage as of MKM release 2024-02-09. The exile-on-a-sacrifice pattern makes it useful in graveyard-recursive black-green shells that want to re-use enter-the-battlefield effects. [Scryfall]

The rose-as-weapon motif (a gorgon using a flower stem to subdue an armored guard) is characteristic of MKM's noir mystery aesthetic — violence that is also aesthetically composed. Art by Quintin Gleim. The gorgon's contemptuous dominance over a heavily armored soldier signals that Golgari exile-status has not diminished their lethality. [Scryfall]

related_cards.name: Vraska, the Unseen
related_cards.note: Canonical Golgari gorgon planeswalker whose Phyrexian compleation led to the Golgari's post-invasion pariah status
